candidate_problems.text: What principled criterion determines the 'appropriate ontological level' of explanation, especially for distinguishing when person-level reasons are genuinely indispensable rather than replaceable by lower-level mechanistic or behavioral models?

How can agential understanding be operationalized or tested without collapsing it into either predictive compression or mere verbal rationalization—i.e., what observable capacities uniquely indicate possession of usable explanatory structure for criticism, transfer, and self-correction?

What is the exact relationship between generative structure and compression: under what conditions does successful compression track real explanatory structure, and when does it instead produce only opaque summaries that systematically miss causal or reason-responsive organization?
